+++ b/scripts/check-gh-token.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+#
+# check-gh-token.sh — Claude Code PreToolUse hook that blocks `gh` and
+# `git push` invocations when GH_TOKEN isn't a bot installation token
+# (ghs_ prefix). Prevents the attribution trap documented in
+# plugin/rules/coordination.md (Token & Git Hygiene) and #140.
+#
+# Hook contract: JSON on stdin, exit 0 = allow, exit 2 = block (stderr
+# is fed back to Claude as the error). See groundnuty/macf#140 for
+# design rationale; 5 recurrences in a single day drove the move from
+# behavioral to structural enforcement.
+#
+# Override: MACF_SKIP_TOKEN_CHECK=1 bypasses (for intentional
+# user-attributed ops, e.g. `gh auth login` during onboarding).
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Operator override first — cheapest exit. No stdin read needed.
+if [[ "${MACF_SKIP_TOKEN_CHECK:-}" == "1" ]]; then
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+# Read and parse the PreToolUse JSON payload. Fall through to allow on
+# parse error — a broken hook must not brick the harness. Claude Code
+# emits a well-formed payload in practice; this is defense-in-depth.
+INPUT_JSON="$(cat)"
+COMMAND="$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' <<<"$INPUT_JSON" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
+
+# Wrapper-aware regex: match `gh ` or `git push ` as the effective
+# command, allowing for zero or more of: sudo, env VAR=VAL..., watch,
+# ionice, setsid, nice, time. Also triggers when preceded by `;`, `&&`,
+# `||`, `|` so chained forms like `make && sudo gh ...` still match.
+# Designed per science-agent's #140 review — the naïve anchored regex
+# was trivially bypassable by wrappers.
+GH_PATTERN='(^|[[:space:];|&])(sudo[[:space:]]+|env[[:space:]]+([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z_0-9]*=[^[:space:]]*[[:space:]]+)*|watch[[:space:]]+|ionice[[:space:]]+|setsid[[:space:]]+|nice[[:space:]]+|time[[:space:]]+|[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z_0-9]*=[^[:space:]]*[[:space:]]+)*(gh[[:space:]]|git[[:space:]]+push([[:space:]]|$))'
+
+if [[ ! "$COMMAND" =~ $GH_PATTERN ]]; then
+  # Not a gh/git-push command — allow without checking token.
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+# Check GH_TOKEN: must be present AND start with ghs_ (bot token).
+# ghp_/gho_/ghu_ are user tokens; empty falls through to stored
+# `gh auth login` (user). Either case fires the trap.
+# Note: `${GH_TOKEN:-}` expansion is mandatory under `set -u`; a bare
+# `${GH_TOKEN:0:4}` errors with "unbound variable" when the env var
+# is unset, which is exactly the case we need to handle.
+GH_TOKEN_VALUE="${GH_TOKEN:-}"
+TOKEN_PREFIX="${GH_TOKEN_VALUE:0:4}"
+if [[ -z "$GH_TOKEN_VALUE" ]] || [[ "$TOKEN_PREFIX" != "ghs_" ]]; then
+  cat >&2 <<ERR
+BLOCKED by MACF attribution-trap hook: this command would post as the USER, not the BOT.
+
+Command: ${COMMAND}
+GH_TOKEN prefix: ${TOKEN_PREFIX:-(empty)}
+
+This hook exists because behavioral controls for the GH_TOKEN attribution
+trap recurred 5 times in a single day. See groundnuty/macf#140 and
+.claude/rules/coordination.md (Token & Git Hygiene).
+
+Fix — refresh the bot token via the fail-loud helper:
+
+  GH_TOKEN=\$(./.claude/scripts/macf-gh-token.sh \\
+    --app-id "\$APP_ID" --install-id "\$INSTALL_ID" --key "\$KEY_PATH") || exit 1
+  export GH_TOKEN
+
+If the helper is missing, restore it:
+  macf rules refresh --dir .
+
+Override (ONLY for intentional user-attributed ops like onboarding):
+  export MACF_SKIP_TOKEN_CHECK=1
+ERR
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+exit 0

+/**
+ * Merge-preserving writer for `<workspace>/.claude/settings.json`.
+ *
+ * Per #140, the attribution-trap PreToolUse hook (`check-gh-token.sh`)
+ * needs a settings.json entry to actually fire. Workspaces may have
+ * operator-authored settings there already (other hooks, env vars,
+ * model preferences) — this module reads the existing JSON (default
+ * `{}`), installs the MACF hook entry, and writes back without
+ * clobbering unrelated keys.
+ *
+ * Identification of MACF-managed entries is by command-string match
+ * on the hook script's filename (`check-gh-token.sh`). A stale entry
+ * from an older CLI version is refreshed in place; non-MACF entries
+ * and other hook event types (SessionStart, Stop, etc.) are preserved
+ * verbatim.
+ *
+ * Malformed settings.json throws — we refuse to silently overwrite
+ * bad JSON because that would erase user content if they hand-edited
+ * broken syntax.
+ */
+import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
+import { join, resolve } from 'node:path';
+
+/**
+ * The command path written into settings.json. Workspace-relative so
+ * it matches what `copyCanonicalScripts` places at
+ * `<workspace>/.claude/scripts/check-gh-token.sh`.
+ */
+export const MACF_HOOK_COMMAND = '.claude/scripts/check-gh-token.sh';
+
+/**
+ * The hook filename used to identify MACF-managed entries on refresh
+ * (so older entries with different flags or paths get replaced
+ * cleanly).
+ */
+const MACF_HOOK_FILENAME = 'check-gh-token.sh';
+
+interface HookCommand {
+  readonly type: 'command';
+  readonly command: string;
+  readonly timeout?: number;
+}
+
+interface HookEntry {
+  readonly matcher?: string;
+  readonly hooks: readonly HookCommand[];
+}
+
+interface Settings {
+  hooks?: {
+    PreToolUse?: HookEntry[];
+    [key: string]: HookEntry[] | undefined;
+  };
+  [key: string]: unknown;
+}
+
+function readSettings(path: string): Settings {
+  if (!existsSync(path)) return {};
+  const raw = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8');
+  try {
+    return JSON.parse(raw) as Settings;
+  } catch (err) {
+    throw new Error(
+      `Refusing to overwrite malformed .claude/settings.json at ${path}: ${(err as Error).message}. ` +
+        `Fix the JSON by hand, then re-run the command.`,
+      { cause: err },
+    );
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Install (or refresh) the MACF PreToolUse hook entry for
+ * `check-gh-token.sh` in `<workspaceDir>/.claude/settings.json`.
+ * Creates the `.claude/` directory and the file if either is missing.
+ *
+ * Idempotent: repeated calls don't duplicate the entry.
+ */
+export function installGhTokenHook(workspaceDir: string): void {
+  const absDir = resolve(workspaceDir);
+  const claudeDir = join(absDir, '.claude');
+  const path = join(claudeDir, 'settings.json');
+
+  mkdirSync(claudeDir, { recursive: true });
+
+  const settings = readSettings(path);
+  const hooks = settings.hooks ?? {};
+  const preToolUse = hooks.PreToolUse ?? [];
+
+  // Drop any prior MACF-managed entries (identified by containing our
+  // hook filename somewhere in a command string) so we can replace
+  // them cleanly — guards against stale flags from older CLI versions.
+  const preserved = preToolUse.filter(
+    (entry) => !entry.hooks.some((h) => h.command.includes(MACF_HOOK_FILENAME)),
+  );
+
+  const macfEntry: HookEntry = {
+    matcher: 'Bash',
+    hooks: [{ type: 'command', command: MACF_HOOK_COMMAND }],
+  };
+
+  const updated: Settings = {
+    ...settings,
+    hooks: {
+      ...hooks,
+      PreToolUse: [...preserved, macfEntry],
+    },
+  };
+
+  writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(updated, null, 2) + '\n');
+}

[{"type":"text","text":"Strong methodological move. Let me think through it before we dig:

## What we'd be looking for (and why each matters)

### Category 1 — defense-firing events (the hook BLOCKED a bad attempt)

The PreToolUse hook (`check-gh-token.sh`) emits a specific stderr signature on exit 2. In session logs, it appears as a Bash tool-use result with:

```
BLOCKED by MACF attribution-trap hook: this command would post as the USER, not the BOT.

Command: <the actual gh/git push line>
GH_TOKEN prefix: <empty | ghp_ | gho_ | ghu_>

This hook exists because behavioral controls...
```

**Why it matters:** every hook-fire event is **direct evidence the structural defense did its job**. A count of these over time gives us:
- "How often did agents *try* to do bot-intent actions with bad tokens?"
- "How many of those attempts would have produced GitHub-side mis-attributions absent the hook?"

This is a stronger claim than "0 mis-attributions in GitHub" alone — it shows the defense ACTIVELY catching attempts, not just that no attempts were made.

### Category 2 — bad-token attempts BEFORE the hook existed (pre-2026-04-21)

Pre-defense, the same pattern would land in session logs differently:
- Bash tool invocation: `gh issue comment ... --body "@macf-code-agent[bot] LGTM"` (or similar bot-intent comment)
- Tool result: exit 0 (silent success — `gh` fell back to stored auth)
- Following turn: agent presumes the comment posted as the bot (no awareness it landed as operator)

**Why it matters:** for each of the 31 pre-#140 GitHub-side events I measured, there should be a corresponding session-log event. Cross-referencing them lets us:
- Forensically verify each GitHub event was an actual trap firing (not legitimate operator action mis-classified by my heuristic)
- See WHICH failure mode (1-6 per `silent-fallback-hazards.md`) fired in each case
- Trace the agent's subsequent behavior — did it notice the mis-attribution and try to fix? Or did it silently proceed?

### Category 3 — failure-mode signatures in token-helper output

The 6 failure modes leave specific traces:

| Mode | Signature in session logs |
|---|---|
| 1. Wrong private key | `macf-gh-token.sh` stderr: "JWT signed with wrong key" or fingerprint mismatch |
| 2. Clock drift | `macf-gh-token.sh` stderr: "JWT could not be decoded" + a hint about clock skew |
| 3. Empty GH_TOKEN → fallback | `gh` invocation with no preceding token-mint step |
| 4. Stored auth login | `ghp_`/`gho_`/`ghu_` prefix mentioned in errors or hook stderr |
| 5. Helper missing | `bash: ./.claude/scripts/macf-gh-token.sh: No such file or directory` |
| 6. Relative path under cd | `macf-gh-token.sh: not found` after a `cd` to another repo |

**Why it matters:** lets us **count the per-failure-mode rate over time**, showing which modes were retired by which defenses (helper hardening, absolute-path fix per #161, etc.). Stronger than aggregate "trap fired" claim.

### Category 4 — hook bypass attempts (regex coverage)

The hook's regex was designed for wrapper-aware matching:
```
(sudo|env [VAR=VAL]+|watch|ionice|setsid|nice|time)*(gh |git push)
```

Pre-#142 (the wrapper-aware version), the regex was naive (`^[[:space:]]*gh `). Bypass forms during that window:
- `sudo gh ...`
- `env GH_TOKEN= gh ...`
- `bash -c "gh ..."` (shell wrapping)
- `GH_TOKEN=x gh ...` (pre-command env assignment)

**Why it matters:** if any session-log event shows a bypass form succeeding with non-bot attribution, that's a CASE STUDY for the iterative-defense-hardening narrative — defense was correct in spirit but escapeable in form, then hardened.

### Category 5 — agent recovery behavior after hook fire

Once the hook blocks, what does the agent do?
- Read the actionable error message (the stderr explains how to refresh the token)
- Run the helper to mint a fresh `ghs_` token
- Retry the original gh command
- Proceeds successfully

**Why it matters:** demonstrates the **closed-loop learning behavior** the structural defense enables. The hook isn't just a block — it's an instructive block that teaches the agent (in-session) how to self-correct. Paper-grade for §3.3 (DD3 structural enforcement claim).

### Category 6 — silent-fallback escapes (the worst case)

The most damning pattern: post-2026-04-21, a session log shows:
- A bot-intent gh command
- Hook DID NOT fire (no BLOCKED stderr)
- gh exited 0
- AND GitHub state shows the action under operator attribution

**Why it matters:** if any of these exist post-#140, they're either (a) hook-bypass cases the regex missed, (b) sessions where MACF_SKIP_TOKEN_CHECK=1 was set, (c) defense-implementation bugs. We need to find these or confirm none exist.

If none exist post-#140 across 18 days × all substrate sessions, that's **the strongest possible empirical claim**: "Instance 1 structurally retired with zero observed escape across N session-hours of post-defense operation."

---

## Where the logs live

| Source | Path | Era | Agents |
|---|---|---|---|
| **VM (this machine)** | `~/.claude/projects/-HOME-repos-groundnuty-macf-science-agent/*.jsonl` | 2026-04-14 onwards | science-agent |
| | `~/.claude/projects/-HOME-repos-groundnuty-macf/*.jsonl` | 2026-03-29 onwards | code-agent |
| | `~/.claude/projects/-HOME-repos-groundnuty-macf-devops-toolkit/*.jsonl` | 2026-04-23 onwards | devops-agent |
| | `~/.claude/projects/-home-ubuntu-tester-{1..6}-home/*.jsonl` | 2026-04-24 onwards | testbed scenario testers (6 ephemeral identities) |
| **Macbook (CPC era)** | `<HOME>/.claude/projects/-Users-<OPERATOR>-repos-hyperflow-1000genome-claude-plan-composer-ts/*.jsonl` | early Feb / March 2026 | cpc-code-agent |
| | `<HOME>/.claude/projects/-Users-<OPERATOR>-repos-papers-claude-plan-composer-paper/*.jsonl` | similar | cpc-science-agent |
| **Macbook (PPAM/CV)** | `<HOME>/.claude/projects/-Users-<OPERATOR>-repos-papers-ppam-2026/*.jsonl` etc. | recent | PPAM + CV agents |

## What I'd propose to investigate (in priority order)

1. **VM substrate logs, post-#140 (2026-04-21 to 2026-05-09)** — count hook-fire events. Highest value, easiest search (grep one literal string). Tells us how much work the defense did.
2. **VM substrate logs, pre-#140 (2026-04-14 to 2026-04-20)** — find the 31 GitHub-side events in their original session contexts. Forensically classify each. Confirm or refute the heuristic count.
3. **Per-failure-mode breakdown** — for both eras, classify each token-helper-error/trap event by which of the 6 failure modes it represents. Shows which defenses retired which modes.
4. **CPC logs (macbook)** — only if CPC's relationship to the paper is still load-bearing. The MACF substrate evidence may be sufficient on its own.
5. **Tester logs (testbed scenarios)** — the 6 ephemeral testers had per-tester GitHub Apps + ran scripted scenarios. Their session logs would show synthetic high-volume gh/git activity with controlled defense state. Could complement the substrate evidence.

## Key risks / things to watch

- **Log volume**: cumulative session JSONL across multiple agents over 41 days could be many GB. Need targeted grep, not full reads.
- **Schema stability**: Claude Code's JSONL schema may have changed between releases. Tool-use result format especially; PreToolUse-hook stderr capture may be in different fields in different versions.
- **MACF_SKIP_TOKEN_CHECK=1 events**: if I (science-agent) used the override during legitimate user-attributed ops, those would look like "hook didn't fire on bad token" but are intentional. Need to filter.
- **Privacy**: session logs contain operator-typed prompts. We'd need to be careful what we publish — only the structured tool-use signatures, not prompt contents.

## Question for you before we start

Three scoping decisions:

**A.** Just VM substrate logs (cleanest, MACF-only, bounded)? Or include macbook (CPC + PPAM/CV)?

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+#
+# check-gh-token.sh — Claude Code PreToolUse hook that blocks `gh` and
+# `git push` invocations when GH_TOKEN isn't a bot installation token
+# (ghs_ prefix). Prevents the attribution trap documented in
+# plugin/rules/coordination.md (Token & Git Hygiene) and #140.
+#
+# Hook contract: JSON on stdin, exit 0 = allow, exit 2 = block (stderr
+# is fed back to Claude as the error). See groundnuty/macf#140 for
+# design rationale; 5 recurrences in a single day drove the move from
+# behavioral to structural enforcement.
+#
+# Override: MACF_SKIP_TOKEN_CHECK=1 bypasses (for intentional
+# user-attributed ops, e.g. `gh auth login` during onboarding).
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Operator override first — cheapest exit. No stdin read needed.
+if [[ "${MACF_SKIP_TOKEN_CHECK:-}" == "1" ]]; then
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+# Read and parse the PreToolUse JSON payload. Fall through to allow on
+# parse error — a broken hook must not brick the harness. Claude Code
+# emits a well-formed payload in practice; this is defense-in-depth.
+INPUT_JSON="$(cat)"
+COMMAND="$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' <<<"$INPUT_JSON" 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
+
+# Wrapper-aware regex: match `gh ` or `git push ` as the effective
+# command, allowing for zero or more of: sudo, env VAR=VAL..., watch,
+# ionice, setsid, nice, time. Also triggers when preceded by `;`, `&&`,
+# `||`, `|` so chained forms like `make && sudo gh ...` still match.
+# Designed per science-agent's #140 review — the naïve anchored regex
+# was trivially bypassable by wrappers.
+GH_PATTERN='(^|[[:space:];|&])(sudo[[:space:]]+|env[[:space:]]+([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z_0-9]*=[^[:space:]]*[[:space:]]+)*|watch[[:space:]]+|ionice[[:space:]]+|setsid[[:space:]]+|nice[[:space:]]+|time[[:space:]]+|[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z_0-9]*=[^[:space:]]*[[:space:]]+)*(gh[[:space:]]|git[[:space:]]+push([[:space:]]|$))'
+
+if [[ ! "$COMMAND" =~ $GH_PATTERN ]]; then
+  # Not a gh/git-push command — allow without checking token.
+  exit 0
+fi
+
+# Check GH_TOKEN: must be present AND start with ghs_ (bot token).
+# ghp_/gho_/ghu_ are user tokens; empty falls through to stored
+# `gh auth login` (user). Either case fires the trap.
+# Note: `${GH_TOKEN:-}` expansion is mandatory under `set -u`; a bare
+# `${GH_TOKEN:0:4}` errors with "unbound variable" when the env var
+# is unset, which is exactly the case we need to handle.
+GH_TOKEN_VALUE="${GH_TOKEN:-}"
+TOKEN_PREFIX="${GH_TOKEN_VALUE:0:4}"
+if [[ -z "$GH_TOKEN_VALUE" ]] || [[ "$TOKEN_PREFIX" != "ghs_" ]]; then
+  cat >&2 <<ERR
+BLOCKED by MACF attribution-trap hook: this command would post as the USER, not the BOT.
+
+Command: ${COMMAND}
+GH_TOKEN prefix: ${TOKEN_PREFIX:-(empty)}
+
+This hook exists because behavioral controls for the GH_TOKEN attribution
+trap recurred 5 times in a single day. See groundnuty/macf#140 and
+.claude/rules/coordination.md (Token & Git Hygiene).
+
+Fix — refresh the bot token via the fail-loud helper:
+
+  GH_TOKEN=\$(./.claude/scripts/macf-gh-token.sh \\
+    --app-id "\$APP_ID" --install-id "\$INSTALL_ID" --key "\$KEY_PATH") || exit 1
+  export GH_TOKEN
+
+If the helper is missing, restore it:
+  macf rules refresh --dir .
+
+Override (ONLY for intentional user-attributed ops like onboarding):
+  export MACF_SKIP_TOKEN_CHECK=1
+ERR
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+exit 0

+/**
+ * Merge-preserving writer for `<workspace>/.claude/settings.json`.
+ *
+ * Per #140, the attribution-trap PreToolUse hook (`check-gh-token.sh`)
+ * needs a settings.json entry to actually fire. Workspaces may have
+ * operator-authored settings there already (other hooks, env vars,
+ * model preferences) — this module reads the existing JSON (default
+ * `{}`), installs the MACF hook entry, and writes back without
+ * clobbering unrelated keys.
+ *
+ * Identification of MACF-managed entries is by command-string match
+ * on the hook script's filename (`check-gh-token.sh`). A stale entry
+ * from an older CLI version is refreshed in place; non-MACF entries
+ * and other hook event types (SessionStart, Stop, etc.) are preserved
+ * verbatim.
+ *
+ * Malformed settings.json throws — we refuse to silently overwrite
+ * bad JSON because that would erase user content if they hand-edited
+ * broken syntax.
+ */
+import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
+import { join, resolve } from 'node:path';
+
+/**
+ * The command path written into settings.json. Workspace-relative so
+ * it matches what `copyCanonicalScripts` places at
+ * `<workspace>/.claude/scripts/check-gh-token.sh`.
+ */
+export const MACF_HOOK_COMMAND = '.claude/scripts/check-gh-token.sh';
+
+/**
+ * The hook filename used to identify MACF-managed entries on refresh
+ * (so older entries with different flags or paths get replaced
+ * cleanly).
+ */
+const MACF_HOOK_FILENAME = 'check-gh-token.sh';
+
+interface HookCommand {
+  readonly type: 'command';
+  readonly command: string;
+  readonly timeout?: number;
+}
+
+interface HookEntry {
+  readonly matcher?: string;
+  readonly hooks: readonly HookCommand[];
+}
+
+interface Settings {
+  hooks?: {
+    PreToolUse?: HookEntry[];
+    [key: string]: HookEntry[] | undefined;
+  };
+  [key: string]: unknown;
+}
+
+function readSettings(path: string): Settings {
+  if (!existsSync(path)) return {};
+  const raw = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8');
+  try {
+    return JSON.parse(raw) as Settings;
+  } catch (err) {
+    throw new Error(
+      `Refusing to overwrite malformed .claude/settings.json at ${path}: ${(err as Error).message}. ` +
+        `Fix the JSON by hand, then re-run the command.`,
+      { cause: err },
+    );
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Install (or refresh) the MACF PreToolUse hook entry for
+ * `check-gh-token.sh` in `<workspaceDir>/.claude/settings.json`.
+ * Creates the `.claude/` directory and the file if either is missing.
+ *
+ * Idempotent: repeated calls don't duplicate the entry.
+ */
+export function installGhTokenHook(workspaceDir: string): void {
+  const absDir = resolve(workspaceDir);
+  const claudeDir = join(absDir, '.claude');
+  const path = join(claudeDir, 'settings.json');
+
+  mkdirSync(claudeDir, { recursive: true });
+
+  const settings = readSettings(path);
+  const hooks = settings.hooks ?? {};
+  const preToolUse = hooks.PreToolUse ?? [];
+
+  // Drop any prior MACF-managed entries (identified by containing our
+  // hook filename somewhere in a command string) so we can replace
+  // them cleanly — guards against stale flags from older CLI versions.
+  const preserved = preToolUse.filter(
+    (entry) => !entry.hooks.some((h) => h.command.includes(MACF_HOOK_FILENAME)),
+  );
+
+  const macfEntry: HookEntry = {
+    matcher: 'Bash',
+    hooks: [{ type: 'command', command: MACF_HOOK_COMMAND }],
+  };
+
+  const updated: Settings = {
+    ...settings,
+    hooks: {
+      ...hooks,
+      PreToolUse: [...preserved, macfEntry],
+    },
+  };
+
+  writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(updated, null, 2) + '\n');
+}
